column4:
All districts except Gadchiroli and 
Bhandara.  Basaltic 
lava 
flows 
consist of two types: 
(a) vesicular/amygdaloidal basalt 
and 
 (b)  Massive  basalt.  Vesicular 
basalt 
possesses 
primary 
porosity 
and 
permeability 
if 
vesicles  are  interconnected  and 
can 
be 
productive 
but  when 
vesicles 
are 
found 
filled  with 
secondary  minerals 
such 
as 
zeolites,  quartz,  calcite  etc.  the 
primary  porosity  of 
the  rock 
is 
reduced 
to 
almost 
nil. 
The 
weathered,  jointed  and  fractured 
parts 
of 
the 
vesicular/amygdaloidal/  massive 
basalt 
formation  constitute 
the 
promising 
water 
bearing 
horizons.

Ratnagiri, 
Chandrapur, 
Yavatmal, 
Gadchiroli, 
Sindhudurg and Wardha districts. 
Aquifer  becomes  productive  by 
fractures and solution cavities.
